primary_reason: The study validates self-reported receipt of eight adult vaccines against electronic medical records and quantifies demographic variation in misclassification.
This two-case melioidosis report describes delayed diagnoses but provides no reusable epidemiologic design or surveillance-correction method.
The study compares standard and single-copy HIV RNA assays and demonstrates systematic undermeasurement near the standard assay's detection limit.
The study applies bias-correction methods to sensitive behavioral reports from a national HIV survey and quantifies behavior-specific underreporting.
This narrative review summarizes Anaplasma ecology, hosts, and transmission without developing or comparing an epidemiologic method.
The study uses event-study and difference-in-difference-in-differences designs to estimate the effect of parental-involvement laws on sexually transmitted infections.
The paper develops stochastic SIR reporting-process models and Bayesian data-augmentation methods for estimating epidemic parameters under underreporting.
The study triangulates hospitalization, laboratory, and notification records to diagnose substantial underreporting of viral encephalitis and meningitis.
The study uses consumer focus groups and clinician interviews to identify behavioral and system barriers causing foodborne-illness underreporting.
The study compares insurance claims with mandatory and voluntary surveillance to quantify large deficits in mumps notification.
The article summarizes measles and rubella trends and anticipated underreporting without presenting a transferable analytic or correction method.
The review systematically audits the inclusion and reporting of women and pregnancy in HIV research, identifying reproducibility and generalizability gaps.

The interrupted time-series evaluates coal controls against predominantly noninfectious mortality and admissions, with influenza used only as a covariate and no concrete infectious-disease transfer demonstrated.
The study reports routine yersiniosis counts and characteristics without a reusable surveillance-adjustment or measurement design.
The study combines outbreak reports, poison-center data, and literature-derived multipliers to correct surveillance undercounts, a structure transferable to infectious foodborne illnesses.
This small clinical series describes pediatric coccidioidomycosis and immune status without a transferable epidemiologic method.
The study systematically reviews vaccine adverse-event reports using expert adjudication and standardized Brighton criteria to estimate hypersensitivity reporting rates.
The study audits historical pneumonia surveillance for reporting bias and uses the resulting data to assess H7N9 epidemiology and poultry-market closures.
The record describes maternal deaths and reporting gaps, with infection only one cause and no concrete infectious-disease methodological transfer specified.
This letter has no supplied abstract or method content beyond asserting underreporting of HIV transmission.
The cross-sectional study uses active interviews to identify unreported occupational exposures to potentially infectious biological material.
The study compares pertussis notifications with hospital-discharge records to derive annual and age-specific minimum underreporting rates.
The door-to-door Buruli-ulcer survey measures community prevalence and directly estimates the coverage failure of hospital-based reporting.
The study combines long-term incident records with an underreporting assessment to measure occupational bloodborne-pathogen exposure among healthcare workers.
These two noninfectious adverse-drug-reaction cases discuss spontaneous-reporting limitations without a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The CDC report gives explicit algorithms for assessing hepatitis B protection and managing occupational exposures among previously vaccinated healthcare personnel.
The study triangulates tetanus notifications, hospitalizations, deaths, and seroprevalence to diagnose underreporting and population susceptibility.
The study fits a mechanistic pertussis transmission model across pre-vaccine and vaccine eras to estimate age-specific underreporting and transmission sources.
This single equine coinfection case describes diagnosis and treatment without a transferable epidemiologic or surveillance design.
The study is a descriptive analysis of traumatic injuries and deaths with no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This prospective spine-surgery complication study concerns a noninfectious intervention and provides no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The study develops and validates three monoclonal-antibody assays for directly detecting low-burden Schistosoma mansoni infection.
The study validates routine malaria microscopy against nested PCR and quantifies false positives, species mismatches, and missed mixed infections.
The paper reviews and compares methods and multiplication factors for estimating infectious-disease under-ascertainment and underreporting.
The study applies number-needed-to-vaccinate analysis with surveillance underreporting adjustments to evaluate a pertussis cocoon policy.
The article prescribes integrated human, animal, foodborne-pathogen, diagnostic, communication, and analytic surveillance approaches for resource-limited settings.
The study combines modeled disease burden with GIS mapping to show how spatial scale and underreporting alter trypanosomiasis priority estimates.
The study combines rodent sampling, molecular detection, phylogenetics, and sequence enrichment to characterize zoonotic Seoul-virus circulation.
The systematic review synthesizes clinical complications of G6PD deficiency to inform primaquine use and diagnostic strategy in malaria-elimination programs.
The study uses stratified negative-binomial time-series regression and an external awareness shock to estimate norovirus underreporting factors.
The study directly compares survey-based and wireless-sensor measurements of contact networks used in infectious-disease transmission models.
The study combines demographic modeling, excess-mortality time series, and district mapping to reconstruct influenza mortality and spatial propagation.
This nutrition review concerns snacking, energy balance, and dietary underreporting without a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The prospective study measures reported and unreported sharps and splash exposures and follows healthcare workers for bloodborne-pathogen seroconversion.
The study links surveillance, mortality, and laboratory data and uses a before-after comparison to assess meningococcal-vaccine impact.
The review corrects circumcision coverage estimates and combines risk-benefit and cost-effectiveness evidence for infection-prevention policy.
The study integrates quasi-Poisson outbreak detection, serotyping, pulsed-field electrophoresis, and whole-genome sequencing in salmonellosis surveillance.
The critical appraisal explains how registry ascertainment, diagnosis, reporting, and background risk bias comparisons of tuberculosis risk across anti-TNF agents.
The paper develops and demonstrates a framework using hospitalization ratios and spatial smoothing to evaluate foodborne-disease surveillance performance.
The study combines self-reported sexual debut with laboratory HPV detection to identify probable behavioral underreporting in adolescents.
The qualitative study uses paired farmer and veterinarian interviews to explain underreporting and poor sensitivity in brucellosis surveillance.
This review addresses complications of a noninfectious sterilization device without a concrete infectious-disease methodological transfer route.
This spine-surgery adverse-event cohort is outside infectious diseases and does not establish a concrete transfer route.
The study develops a nested quantitative-PCR approach and evaluates why standard qPCR misses low-titer plant-pathogen infections, a concrete low-load pathogen-detection method.
The study validates parental HPV-vaccination reports against provider histories and quantifies socially patterned false-positive and false-negative reports.
The study compares adolescent self-reported lifetime sexually transmitted infections with electronic medical records to detect pathogen-specific underreporting.
This single camel anaplasmosis case documents diagnosis and treatment without a transferable epidemiologic method.
The paper summarizes national HIV case-reporting counts and possible transmission-category underreporting without applying a correction or validation method.
The review synthesizes alternative underreporting-adjusted burden estimates with ecological, climatic, and mobility determinants of cholera transmission in Africa.
This descriptive pedestrian-injury study is outside infectious diseases and supplies no concrete transfer route.
This gastric-cancer management review is primarily noninfectious and does not present a concrete infectious-disease methodological transfer.
The study synthesizes articles, reports, and WHO data to characterize cholera outbreak sources, strains, resistance, and regional patterns.
The nationwide medical-record review validates STEC and hemolytic-uremic-syndrome surveillance and measures sensitivity before and after a case-definition change.

The study uses probabilistic linkage between notification and mortality databases to identify unreported congenital-syphilis outcomes.
The study combines cattle performance data with spatiotemporal geostatistical interpolation to infer unreported bluetongue-affected herds.
This case review catalogs European mycetoma organisms and treatments but does not develop a transferable surveillance or analytic method.
The article argues for more humane canine-leishmaniasis control and additional research without providing enough methodological content.
The population survey estimates acute-gastroenteritis incidence, care seeking, economic burden, and surveillance underreporting in Saint Lucia.
The study combines population and laboratory surveys to estimate community gastroenteritis burden and the multiplier from confirmed reports to population cases.
The study uses seasonal population surveys and pathogen testing to estimate gastroenteritis incidence, underreporting, and economic burden.
The study combines population and laboratory surveillance to derive syndromic and pathogen-confirmed gastroenteritis underreporting factors.
The population survey estimates acute-gastroenteritis incidence, surveillance multipliers, laboratory gaps, and economic burden in Guyana.
The cohort study uses maximum-likelihood estimation and sensitivity analysis for underreported equipment sharing to estimate per-event HCV transmission risk.
The representative One Health serosurvey uses cluster sampling and a catalytic model to infer human brucellosis incidence and underreporting.

The study compares national hospitalization and notification datasets to reassess underreporting after changes in infectious-disease reporting infrastructure.
The study experimentally compares cell lines and demonstrates that isolation-protocol choice causes underdetection of nonpolio enteroviruses.

The physician survey measures knowledge, attitudes, and operational barriers associated with infectious-disease underreporting.
The mechanistic model combines adult prevalence, pediatric natural history, household exposure, vaccination, and HIV to estimate childhood tuberculosis burden.
The article advocates an integrated response to neglected infections but supplies no substantive method development, comparison, or diagnostic framework.
The cross-sectional study reports correlates of provider choice among brucellosis patients using ordinary regression without a reusable methodological contribution.
The article presents routine annual salmonellosis counts and characteristics without implementing a surveillance-correction method.
This prospective oncology spine-surgery safety study is outside infectious diseases and provides no concrete transfer route.
The review synthesizes rabies epidemiology, diagnosis, vaccination, immunoglobulin use, and animal-control measures into prevention guidance.
This descriptive scorpion-envenomation series concerns a toxin-mediated condition and gives no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.

The paper develops maternal-mortality correction estimates, while H1N1 only explains one temporal fluctuation and no infectious-disease transfer route is established.
The active pediatric pharmacovigilance program captures vaccine and drug adverse events and evaluates a strategy for reducing reporting deficits.

The study describes norovirus prevalence, genotypes, and seasonality in submitted outbreak specimens without a distinct transferable method contribution.

The EbolaResponse transmission model projects cases under underreporting corrections and simulates how treatment and isolation scale-up changes epidemic trajectories.
This cellular immunology study develops an IL-22 reporter mouse but does not provide a concrete epidemiologic-method transfer despite including an infection experiment.
The multicenter worker survey estimates occupational blood exposure, protocol underreporting, and hepatitis B vaccination gaps.
The systematic review identifies recurrent surgical-instrument decontamination failures and relates them to infection-control and sterilization guidance.

The study uses viral sequence phylogenies and branch lengths to reconstruct cross-border poliovirus transmission and identify surveillance gaps.
The study triangulates national morbidity, hospitalization, mortality, and literature records to quantify tularemia underreporting.

The study uses Monte Carlo simulation within a benefit-cost framework to evaluate a national foot-and-mouth-disease vaccination program.
This diagnostic meta-analysis evaluates lung-cancer staging, and its consideration of coexisting infection does not establish a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The study estimates cervical-cancer incidence from registry data but does not analyze HPV transmission or provide a concrete infectious-disease method transfer.
The study validates self-reported alcohol use against phosphatidylethanol among people starting HIV treatment and identifies patterned underreporting relevant to HIV research.
This ciguatera review concerns marine toxins rather than infection and does not present a concrete infectious-disease methodological transfer.
The critical review compares influenza burden models, diagnoses confounding and outcome-misclassification problems, and prescribes validation criteria for vaccine-effectiveness estimates.

The paper develops exceedance probabilities from hierarchical spatiotemporal models as a method for detecting anomalously low disease reporting.

The study validates registry-recorded RSV prophylaxis against medical records and quantifies indication-specific undercapture of palivizumab use.

The study modifies the self-controlled case-series method for underreported spontaneous reports and evaluates its use in rotavirus-vaccine safety.

The matched case-control study reports behavioral correlates of salmonellosis using standard regression without a distinct transferable methodological contribution.
The prospective study validates repeated sexual-behavior reports against a semen biomarker and shows that misreporting changes over follow-up.

The deterministic SEIR model estimates Ebola reproduction numbers from corrected and uncorrected case data and evaluates isolation requirements.

The study comprehensively evaluates brucellosis surveillance using data-quality review, capture-recapture sensitivity estimation, and qualitative acceptability assessment.

The study uses two independent surveillance-based approaches, including the Lives Saved Tool, to estimate vaccination impact and cost-effectiveness.
